# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 145

This is turn 145. Turns 1 through 144 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 145.

## Turn 145 — Proposition I passes an independent characteristic-boundary audit

The frozen \(h_0\) submission and certificate schema remain unchanged. Turn 142 isolated the marked boundary as Proposition I, and Turn 144 left independent review of Propositions I–III as the main non-verifier gap.  

### Exact specialization of the submitted word

Let
\[
W_h=\langle\!\langle x_0,x_1\rangle\!\rangle.
\]

Modulo \(W_h\), both \(x_i\) vanish and \(\tau^{\omega_2}=1\), so every auxiliary word \(u_i,d_0,z_0,c_0,dg,hcomm,h_0\) is trivial. Hence
\[
\Gamma_h/W_h\cong
T=\langle\sigma,\tau\mid \tau^\sigma=\tau^2\rangle.
\]

In every pro-\(2\) quotient,
\[
\tau=1,\qquad \sigma_2=\sigma,\qquad g_0=\sigma^2,\qquad
u_i=x_i,\qquad d_0=c_0=dg=hcomm=1.
\]
Thus
\[
h_0=x_0^{\sigma^2}x_0,
\qquad
u_1^{-1}x_1^\sigma=x_1^{-1}x_1^\sigma=[x_1,\sigma],
\]
and the wild relation becomes exactly
\[
r_*:=x_0^{\sigma^2}x_0[x_1,\sigma]=1.
\]
Therefore
\[
\Gamma_h(2)\cong
\Pi_*=
\left\langle\sigma,x_0,x_1\ \middle|\ 
x_0^{\sigma^2}x_0[x_1,\sigma]=1
\right\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}-2}.
\]

This is equality of the full marked pro-\(2\) boundary, not merely agreement of low Zassenhaus jets.

### Direct verification from the standard local relation

Use
\[
[x,y]=x^{-1}y^{-1}xy,\qquad x^g=g^{-1}xg.
\]
Start from the standard local presentation
\[
D_{\mathrm{std}}
=
\langle a,b,c\mid a^2b^4[b,c]=1\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}-2}
\]
and set
\[
\sigma=b,\qquad x_0=(ab^2)^{-1},\qquad x_1=c.
\]
Then \(a=x_0^{-1}\sigma^{-2}\), so
\[
a^2b^4
=x_0^{-1}\sigma^{-2}x_0^{-1}\sigma^2
=x_0^{-1}(x_0^{-1})^{\sigma^2}.
\]
Consequently
\[
r_{\mathrm{std}}
=x_0^{-1}(x_0^{-1})^{\sigma^2}[\sigma,x_1].
\]
After inversion,
\[
r_{\mathrm{std}}^{-1}
=[x_1,\sigma]x_0^{\sigma^2}x_0,
\]
whose cyclic conjugate is precisely \(r_*\). Thus the two relators have the same closed normal closure. This verifies the change of generators used when the old \(A_2\) boundary was retired. 

The marking is also exact. From
\[
\nu(a)=-2,\qquad \nu(b)=1,\qquad \nu(c)=0
\]
we obtain
\[
\nu(\sigma)=1,\qquad \nu(x_0)=\nu(x_1)=0.
\]
The intrinsic orientation becomes
\[
\chi(\sigma)=1,\qquad
\chi(x_0)=-1,\qquad
\chi(x_1)=(-3)^{-1}.
\]
Moreover,
\[
\Pi_*^{\mathrm{ab}}
\cong
\mathbf Z_2\bar\sigma\oplus
\mathbf Z_2\bar x_1\oplus
C_2\bar x_0.
\]
The pair \((\nu,\chi)\) is injective: \(\nu\) detects the \(\bar\sigma\)-coordinate, the sign of \(\chi\) detects \(\bar x_0\), and \(-3\equiv5\pmod8\) topologically generates \(1+4\mathbf Z_2\), detecting \(\bar x_1\). The marking defect that killed the old \(A_2\) word therefore cannot recur here.

### The wild subgroup is characteristic

The remaining point is to show that the marking is intrinsic rather than an arbitrary choice of generators.

Let \(I=\overline{\langle\tau\rangle}\triangleleft T\), the pro-odd tame-inertia subgroup. Suppose \(N\triangleleft T\) is pro-\(2\). Then
\[
N\cap I=1,\qquad [N,I]\le N\cap I=1,
\]
so \(N\) centralizes tame inertia.

The image of \(N\) in \(T/I\) has a \(2\)-adic Frobenius coordinate \(a\in\mathbf Z_2\). Centralizing every cyclic inertia quotient \(C_e\) forces
\[
2^a\equiv1\pmod e.
\]
For
\[
e_m=2^{2^m}-1,
\]
one has
\[
\operatorname{ord}_{e_m}(2)=2^m:
\]
the exponent \(2^m\) works, while no smaller positive exponent can work because \(2^d-1<e_m\) for \(d<2^m\). Hence \(a\in2^m\mathbf Z_2\) for every \(m\), so \(a=0\). Thus \(N\subseteq I\), and therefore \(N=1\). Hence
\[
O_2(T)=1.
\]

Because \(W_h\) is normal pro-\(2\) and \(\Gamma_h/W_h\cong T\),
\[
W_h=O_2(\Gamma_h).
\]
The identical argument for
\[
1\longrightarrow P_{\mathrm{wild}}
\longrightarrow G_{\mathbf Q_2}
\longrightarrow T
\longrightarrow1
\]
gives
\[
P_{\mathrm{wild}}=O_2(G_{\mathbf Q_2}).
\]
Thus both marked wild subgroups are characteristic.

Finally, the only common elementary \(2\)-quotient of \(T\) and
\(\Pi_*^{\mathrm{el}}\cong C_2^3\) is the parity of \(\sigma\). Therefore the corrected complete boundary is canonically
\[
\partial_*=
T\times_{C_2}\Pi_*^{\mathrm{el}},
\]
exactly the boundary consumed by the terminal recurrence branch.

**ESTABLISHED**: Independently proved Proposition I in its characteristic form. The \(h_0\) word has tame quotient \(T\), marked maximal pro-\(2\) quotient \(\Pi_*\), and the change from the standard local Demuškin generators preserves both the unramified marking and cyclotomic orientation. Proved uniformly that \(O_2(T)=1\), hence \(W_h=O_2(\Gamma_h)\) and local wild inertia \(=O_2(G_{\mathbf Q_2})\); the common boundary \(\partial_*\) is therefore intrinsic.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. Propositions II and III still lack an independent line-by-line audit comparable to this one.

**NEXT**: Audit Proposition II at the chain level: rederive the finite-Heisenberg adjoint identity directly from the exact \(h_0\) word, then verify that the mapping-cone dévissage is natural for arbitrary nonsemisimple elementary modules.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
